description: No reliable K=128 anchor exists in memory yet. This round should spend one proxy run on an explicitly pinned mainline topk baseline so subsequent architecture work can target material FVU improvement rather than comparing against an implicit or drifting default.
self_review: This is intentionally conservative, but the memory is empty and the primary objective depends on having a trustworthy K=128 reference. I avoided architecture edits because there is not yet enough evidence to justify a new family over first establishing the anchor.

description: The K=128 topk full baseline is now anchored at FVU 0.1853. The next round should probe a more expressive encoder family at the same K rather than shrinking K or spending another round on local topk tuning.
self_review: This is the right point to open architecture exploration, but it is still a first incubation pass, so the result may be noisy or need follow-up tuning before judging the family. I kept the rest of the setup close to the anchor to preserve interpretability.

description: mixture_topk was slightly worse than the topk anchor under normal runtime, so the next move is to try a different expressive family rather than spending another round on that branch. residual_topk is the most directly FVU-oriented next architecture because it explicitly allocates encoder capacity to residual errors.
self_review: This keeps the comparison clean and opens a new family with a stronger direct link to reconstruction quality than mixture routing. It is still a first prototype pass, so a mediocre result should not automatically rule out all multi-branch encoders.

description: Both mixture_topk and residual_topk archived under normal runtime with proxy FVU near the topk proxy anchor, so the next move is another architecture family rather than smaller-K search. factorized_topk is the clearest remaining low-friction expressivity upgrade in the current codebase.
self_review: This is still exploratory, but it continues the right search pattern: hold K fixed, change one architectural idea, and see whether encoder expressivity can move proxy FVU. If this also comes back flat, the current built-in families are not yet showing a path to the target.

description: The first factorized_topk prototype was much worse than the anchor, but its implementation used a plain hidden layer with width equal to d_in, which is a likely bottleneck rather than a decisive family verdict. This round keeps the same family and K while upgrading the encoder to a wider gated mixer.
self_review: This is a coherent follow-up on the only family that produced a strongly different signal, and it targets an obvious implementation bottleneck instead of abandoning the branch too early. The edit increases encoder compute, so even a quality gain still needs later cost scrutiny.

description: Round 5 produced a shape-mismatch crash inside factorized_topk after the widening edit, so the next move is a direct bug-fix rerun rather than abandoning the family. This keeps the hypothesis narrow: validate the intended wider gated factorized encoder before making any new architectural decision.
self_review: This is the correct response to the last round because the failure was a concrete code bug, not a training-quality outcome. The rerun is still only worth one more round; if the fixed version remains poor, this branch should be deprioritized.

description: factorized_topk improved from its worst prototype but is still far from the K=128 anchor, and the current built-in families are not showing a path to the target. This round opens a new family designed to be safer than pure nonlinear encoders by keeping the baseline linear path intact and only learning a nonlinear correction on top.
self_review: This is a broader architecture move, but it is still a single coherent bet: combine the proven linear encoder with a nonlinear residual branch instead of replacing it. The new family adds compute, so even a promising quality signal would need later cost validation.

description: hybrid_topk is the first family to improve the K=128 full frontier, but the gain is still small relative to the aggressive quality target. This round keeps the architecture fixed and changes only the optimizer to probe whether the family is currently undertrained rather than intrinsically capped.
self_review: This is a disciplined follow-up because the family finally showed real full-run promise and deserves one quality-focused stabilization pass before opening more architectures. Adam may not help, but it tests a concrete training-dynamics hypothesis without confounding the model design.

description: The hybrid_topk Adam stabilization produced a meaningful full improvement to 0.1619 FVU, but that is still far from the aggressive target. This round keeps the best family and optimizer fixed and spends one proxy run on a larger expansion factor as the cleanest quality-first scaling test.
self_review: This is the right next bet because the current mainline finally has momentum and deserves capacity scaling before any lower-K or cost tradeoffs. The main risk is that extra capacity may give only marginal quality gains relative to added memory and compute.

description: The expansion_factor=16 follow-up did not improve full FVU and cost substantial throughput, so the next move should be a targeted architecture refinement rather than more brute-force scaling. This round keeps the winning hybrid_topk Adam ef=8 setup and upgrades the nonlinear branch to modulate linear logits as well as add residual logits.
self_review: This is a narrow, defensible follow-up on the current best family and it directly addresses the likely limit of the present hybrid design without reopening the broader search space. It still adds encoder complexity, so any quality gain will need later cost scrutiny against the ef=8 Adam mainline.

description: The latest hybrid_topk edit was a normal-runtime regression, and several rounds have now been spent on local hybrid stabilization without another gain. This round shifts back to architecture exploration with an untried family whose learned thresholds could improve selectivity at the same K=128 anchor point.
self_review: This is a deliberate reset to a genuinely different sparse activation mechanism after the hybrid local edits stalled. The risk is that JumpReLU may proxy poorly under the fixed-K implementation, but it is the cleanest untried family with a credible path to lower FVU.

description: The first jumprelu proxy was slightly better than the recent hybrid proxy points, but not strong enough to justify an immediate full run. This round keeps the new family and optimizer fixed and tunes its threshold dynamics directly, which is the most important family-specific lever for quality.
self_review: This is the right follow-up because JumpReLU's distinctive mechanism is its learned thresholding, so the defaults deserve at least one targeted pass before judging the family. The chosen threshold and bandwidth may still be off, but the hypothesis is clean and easy to evaluate.
